stem: A single Toffoli `ccx(0, 1, 2)` is transpiled onto a 3-qubit LINE backend (basis {cx, rz, sx, x}; connectivity 0-1-2). How many two-qubit (`cx`) gates does the resulting ISA circuit contain?
code:
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit
from qiskit.transpiler import CouplingMap
from qiskit.transpiler.preset_passmanagers import generate_preset_pass_manager
from qiskit.providers.fake_provider import GenericBackendV2

backend = GenericBackendV2(3, coupling_map=CouplingMap.from_line(3), seed=7)
qc = QuantumCircuit(3)
qc.ccx(0, 1, 2)

isa = generate_preset_pass_manager(1, backend, seed_transpiler=7).run(qc)
print(isa.count_ops().get('cx'))

options.text: 0 — the backend runs the 3-qubit Toffoli directly.
6 or more CX — the Toffoli is decomposed into many one- and two-qubit basis gates, and routing on a line adds still more.

explanation.correct: `ccx` is neither a basis gate nor a two-qubit gate, so it is decomposed. A Toffoli already needs 6 CX in an all-to-all decomposition, and routing it on a linear coupling map inserts additional CX (via SWAPs), giving well over 6 CX in practice.

distractors.A: `ccx` is not in the basis and acts on 3 qubits; hardware cannot run it directly, so it must be decomposed into CX-based gates.
distractors.B: One CX is nowhere near enough to realize a Toffoli — the standard decomposition alone needs 6.
distractors.C: The minimal Toffoli decomposition already uses 6 CX; on a line with routing the count is even higher, so it is not in the 2-5 range.

verification.proof_script:
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit
from qiskit.transpiler import CouplingMap
from qiskit.transpiler.preset_passmanagers import generate_preset_pass_manager
from qiskit.providers.fake_provider import GenericBackendV2

backend = GenericBackendV2(3, coupling_map=CouplingMap.from_line(3), seed=7)
qc = QuantumCircuit(3)
qc.ccx(0, 1, 2)
isa = generate_preset_pass_manager(1, backend, seed_transpiler=7).run(qc)
ncx = isa.count_ops().get("cx", 0)
claims = {
    "A": ncx == 0,
    "B": ncx == 1,
    "C": 2 <= ncx <= 5,
    "D": ncx >= 6,
}
evidence = {
    "A": f"observed cx count={ncx}; claim 0 -> {claims['A']}",
    "B": f"observed cx count={ncx}; claim 1 -> {claims['B']}",
    "C": f"observed cx count={ncx}; claim 2-5 -> {claims['C']}",
    "D": f"observed cx count={ncx}; claim >=6 -> {claims['D']}",
}
proven = [k for k, v in claims.items() if v]
emit_verdict({"answer": proven, "evidence": evidence, "observed": {"cx_count": ncx, "ops": dict(isa.count_ops())}})
